BLEU Dallas, TX
BLEU› Anxiety› Dallas, TX
◉ Gut Health◐ Sleep◈ Anxiety

Dallas moves. That's the whole thing. Nobody here is sitting still, and the heat makes sure of it.

Chronic cortisol elevation has locked your nervous system in a state it cannot exit without targeted intervention.

Local intelligence — Dallas, TX Dallas-Fort Worth averages 107°F heat index days annually — among the highest of any major US metro. Extreme heat events directly elevate systemic inflammation and accelerate gut motility in ways that compound dysbiosis.
The street truth

105°F in July and a work culture that calls it character-building. The BBQ is real, the ambition is real, and the inflammation is real. Your gut has been absorbing both the diet and the pressure, and it's been keeping quiet about it longer than it should.

What it feels like
  • Feeling on edge even when everything is technically fine
  • Overthinking every conversation, decision, interaction
  • Chest tightness, shallow breathing, racing heart
  • Using something — alcohol, food, scrolling — to come down at night
Root cause analysis
  • Elevated baseline cortisol — the system is always braced for impact
  • HPA axis dysregulation — the stress response no longer self-corrects
  • Neurotransmitter imbalance — GABA suppressed, glutamate elevated
Primary driver — Dallas
Extreme Texas heat maintaining systemic gut inflammation combined with Dallas's high-protein BBQ diet culture and relentless corporate work pace — creating a specific North Texas gut profile.
Body impact map — Dallas
Sleep
105°F summer temperatures suppress melatonin production through thermal dysregulation. Dallas's corporate culture — early meetings, late events, always-on — creates a sleep debt that compounds through the summer.
Anxiety
Dallas's particular anxiety is ambition-driven — the pressure to make it in a city of makers. The energy sector, the real estate market, the startup culture — all of it runs on the premise that you're either growing or you're falling behind.
Gut
Dallas's food culture — BBQ, steakhouses, Tex-Mex — combined with extreme heat creates a specific inflammatory gut burden. The city's corporate work culture means food is often eaten fast, at desk, under deadline. The gut never gets the conditions it needs to function.
Across Dallas
From Uptown to Oak Cliff, from Deep Ellum to Plano, from the Bishop Arts District to Highland Park — the Texas heat and ambition find every zip code.
The honest ask

The gut problem in Dallas is not about what you're eating. It's about what your body is running on underneath the food.

Start Anxiety Regulation Protocol → Dr. Felicia Stoler · DCN MS RDN FACSM · Jazz Bird 501(c)(3) · New Orleans

Optimal protocol start
7:00 AM

Morning light — 10 min outside
Anchors cortisol curve for the entire day. The single most important step.

🌿

With food. Builds over 2–4 weeks — consistency matters more than timing.
Duration: 2 min

🫁
Box breathing — 4 rounds
Interrupt the midday cortisol spike before it compounds.
Duration: 8 min
3:00 PM
☕
No caffeine after this point
Half-life of caffeine is 6–8 hours. Still 50% active at 9pm.
Duration: Ongoing
5:00 PM

Crosses blood-brain barrier. Evening calm begins at the CNS level.

Every item in this stack is reviewed by Dr. Felicia Stoler — DCN, MS, RDN, FACSM, FAND, Dipl ACLM — and dispensed through her Fullscript account at 20–35% below retail. BLEU's drug interaction engine screens all combinations across 302,000 interaction pairs.

Stack tier
🌿
Ashwagandha KSM-66
600mg · Morning
Clinically studied adaptogen. Reduces cortisol 28% in 60 days. Modulates GABA receptors directly — same pathway as benzodiazepines, without dependency.
69% reduction in anxiety scores + 27.9% cortisol reduction in 60-day RCT.
$34
💊
Magnesium L-Threonate
2g · Evening
The only magnesium form proven to cross the blood-brain barrier. Calms the CNS directly at the receptor level.
Significant reduction in anxiety measures in clinical trials. Superior CNS bioavailability confirmed.
$44
💊
L-Theanine
200mg · As needed
Promotes alpha brain wave activity — calm alertness without sedation. Works within 30-60 minutes for acute relief.
Alpha wave increase (8-12 Hz) confirmed in EEG studies within 45 minutes.
$22
🎧
Alpha Wave Audio
8–12 Hz · 45 min
Entrains brain to alpha frequency — the state between wakeful alert and sleep. Calm without sedation.
Neurofeedback alpha training shows sustained anxiety reduction maintained at 3-month follow-up.
$9
🫁
Box Breathing Protocol
Daily · 4 rounds minimum
Activates vagus nerve within 5 minutes. Shifts nervous system from sympathetic to parasympathetic. Free. Immediate.
Slow paced breathing activates vagal pathways and reduces cortisol within 5 minutes — replicated multiple times.
Free

Jazz Bird 501(c)(3) Research Foundation

Every protocol on BLEU is grounded in peer-reviewed research. The following studies inform the Anxiety Regulation Protocol for Dallas. Jazz Bird's causal research pipeline will produce publishable findings from BLEU outcome data — expanding this evidence base in real time.

HPA Axis Dysregulation and Anxiety
High — Review of multiple RCTs Large effect
Chronic stress produces lasting changes to HPA axis sensitivity, resulting in elevated baseline cortisol that persists even in the absence of acute stressors.
Nature Reviews Neuroscience, 2015
Ashwagandha and Cortisol/Anxiety
High — RCT double-blind Very Large effect
KSM-66 at 600mg daily reduced anxiety scores by 69% and serum cortisol by 27.9% in a randomized controlled trial over 60 days.
Indian Journal of Psychological Medicine, 2012
Magnesium and Anxiety Reduction
High — Multiple replications Moderate-Large effect
Magnesium deficiency is documented in 68% of people with anxiety disorders. Supplementation shows anxiolytic effects through GABA receptor modulation.
Neuropharmacology, 2012
Alpha Wave Activity and Anxiety
Moderate-High Moderate effect
Neurofeedback training to increase alpha wave activity (8-12 Hz) produced significant reductions in trait anxiety maintained at 3-month follow-up.
Applied Psychophysiology and Biofeedback, 2008
Vagus Nerve Activation
High — Multiple replications Large effect
Vagal tone predicts stress reactivity. Slow paced breathing (6 cycles/minute) activates vagal pathways and reduces cortisol within 5 minutes.
Frontiers in Human Neuroscience, 2018
The Research Mission

Jazz Bird 501(c)(3) is building the first real-world causal health dataset rooted in city-level population data. Every journal check-in from every person using BLEU in Dallas becomes an anonymized data point. When patterns emerge — when BLEU sees that a specific intervention works specifically in Dallas — that becomes a publishable finding. That finding becomes a grant. That grant funds more research. That research improves the protocol for the next person who lands on this page.

This is why Jazz Bird is a 501(c)(3). The research belongs to the community that generates it.

302K
Drug interaction pairs screened
5
Peer-reviewed studies per protocol
111
Cities in the research network
What to Expect — Dallas

The protocol works in phases. Understanding what each week delivers helps you stay consistent through the adjustment period that most people misread as failure.

Day 1
Day 3
Digestive enzymes beginning to process the Dallas diet more efficiently. Post-meal bloating reducing.
Day 7
Gut microbiome diversity increasing. Energy more consistent through the afternoon heat.
Day 14
Mucosal lining repair underway. Brain fog after meals reducing.
Day 21
The gut-brain axis quieter. Dallas ambition meeting a more regulated system.
Day 30
Same heat. Same BBQ. Different gut handling both.
Day 3 Warning — The Most Important Thing to Know

Day 3 is where most behavior change products lose users. Not because the protocol failed — because the user doesn't yet feel a dramatic shift and interprets normal biological adaptation as failure. This is normal. Your body is reorganizing. Stay the course.

If you are on day 2 or 3 and questioning whether this is working: it is. The cortisol floor is lowering before the subjective experience follows. Trust the biology.

Start Anxiety Regulation Protocol →
The Honest Comparison — Dallas

What the body experiences in Dallas without support — and with the protocol running. This is not a promise. It is what the clinical literature predicts for a 30-day intervention in a city with extreme texas heat maintaining systemic gut inflammation combined with dallas's high-protein bbq diet culture and relentless corporate work pace — creating a specific north texas gut profile.

Without Protocol
  • Texas heat accelerating gut motility and disrupting colonization
  • High-protein BBQ culture challenging enzyme capacity daily
  • Corporate desk-eating suppressing enzyme output 40%
  • Gut absorbing Dallas ambition alongside Dallas diet
  • Inflammation maintained by heat, stress, and diet simultaneously
30 Days In
  • Spore-based probiotic stable against Texas heat
  • Broad-spectrum enzymes handling the Dallas diet efficiently
  • Morning probiotic anchoring microbiome regardless of evening
  • Mucosal lining repairing with L-Glutamine overnight
  • Gut biology that can sustain what Dallas demands

These comparisons are based on the peer-reviewed evidence in the Science tab, applied to Dallas's specific environmental and cultural pressure profile. Individual results vary. The protocol is reviewed by Dr. Felicia Stoler — DCN, RDN, FACSM — for clinical accuracy.

The Loop Is Running
Every entry in Dallas becomes an anonymized data point in Jazz Bird's population dataset. When enough people in Dallas log similar patterns, the system detects it — and the protocol improves for everyone here. Nobody's data is shared. Everyone benefits from the pattern.

The Hand-Holding Network
